stem: Your circuit leaves some qubits idle for long stretches, and you want to suppress decoherence on them during execution. Which EstimatorV2 option should you enable?

explanation.correct: Dynamical decoupling inserts pulse sequences on idle qubits to approximately cancel decoherence while they wait — exactly the situation described. It is an error-suppression technique enabled via `options.dynamical_decoupling.enable = True`.

distractors.A: ZNE is a post-processing error-mitigation method for expectation values; it does not protect idle qubits during execution.
distractors.B: `measure_mitigation` (TREX) corrects readout errors on measurement, not decoherence on idle qubits.
distractors.D: Resilience level 1 enables readout mitigation (TREX), not dynamical decoupling; DD is configured through its own option group.
